issue.title: Biostatistics discovery must handle per-volume metrics from remote MLflow

schema.name: analysis_results.duckdb
schema.description: Raw experimental results materialized from MLflow. Self-contained: a biostatistician receives THIS file alone and can reproduce all analyses. Extends existing biostatistics_duckdb.py schema (no parallel module).

schema.name: biostatistics.duckdb
schema.description: Statistical analysis results + diagnostics. Visualization developers receive THIS file + JSON sidecars to create publication figures.

phase.name: Per-Volume Metrics Pipeline
phase.gate: analysis_results.duckdb exists with non-empty per_volume_metrics table. At least 8 runs × 2 folds × 15 metrics. All 4 conditions distinguishable. DuckDB round-trips correctly.

task.name: Retroactively tag DagsHub runs + fix training flow
task.description:
The 8 existing DagsHub runs lack with_aux_calib tag. Use MlflowClient.set_tag() to retroactively add all factorial metadata. Also fix training_flow.py to log with_aux_calib for future runs.
Config → tag mapping: local_dynunet_dice_ce → loss_function=dice_ce, with_aux_calib=false local_dynunet_dice_ce_auxcalib → loss_function=dice_ce, with_aux_calib=true local_dynunet_cbdice_cldice → loss_function=cbdice_cldice, with_aux_calib=false local_dynunet_cbdice_cldice_auxcalib → loss_function=cbdice_cldice, with_aux_calib=true All: model_family=dynunet, post_training_method=none, is_zero_shot=false
Retroactive tagging script: DELETE-AFTER-USE (Rule 26 — one-off migration). Or better: implement as a Prefect task within the analysis flow.
PREREQUISITE: .env must have DAGSHUB_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME for the MLflow client to connect.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test set_factorial_tags() applies correct tags given run_id + config map. RED: Test training_flow logs with_aux_calib tag. GREEN: Implement using MlflowClient.set_tag().
task.acceptance: All 8 DagsHub runs have correct tags (4 distinct conditions). discover_source_runs_from_api() returns 4 conditions. Training flow logs with_aux_calib for future runs. Tests: >= 4.

task.name: Extend biostatistics_duckdb.py with analysis DuckDB builder
task.description:
COUNCIL FIX: Extend the EXISTING biostatistics_duckdb.py module (Rule 26 — no parallel modules). Add build_analysis_results_duckdb() function.
build_analysis_results_duckdb( runs: list[SourceRun], per_volume_data: dict, # From FoldResult.per_volume_metrics (already exists!) fold_metrics: dict, output_path: Path, metadata: dict[str, str], # Includes full BiostatisticsConfig JSON ) -> Path
Creates analysis_results.duckdb with 4 tables (runs, per_volume_metrics, fold_metrics, metadata). NO separate calibration table — calibration metrics go into per_volume_metrics with cal_ prefix. NO training_curves table (available in MLflow, not needed for statistics).
Also exports all tables to Parquet at output_path.parent / "parquet/". NO condition_key in metric tables — only in runs table.
PURE function — no MLflow, no Prefect dependency.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test build_analysis_results_duckdb() creates 4-table DuckDB from mock data. RED: Test Parquet export — all .parquet files exist. RED: Test round-trip: write → read → data matches. RED: Test condition_key ONLY in runs table, NOT in metric tables. RED: Test metadata has biostatistics_config_json key. GREEN: Implement in biostatistics_duckdb.py using duckdb.connect().
task.acceptance: DuckDB with 4 tables, correct schema. Parquet export for all tables. Round-trip integrity verified. Tests: >= 8.

task.name: Wire task_export_analysis_duckdb() into Analysis Flow
task.description:
Add Prefect task to Analysis Flow: 1. Collects per-volume results from upstream evaluate tasks (FoldResult.per_volume_metrics already exists — this is WIRING, not computation) 2. Calls build_analysis_results_duckdb() 3. Logs DuckDB as MLflow artifact on DagsHub 4. Returns output path
Wire after per-fold evaluations, before champion selection: evaluate_all_models() → task_export_analysis_duckdb() → register_champion_task() (Using actual task names from analysis_flow.py, not idealized names.)
Update docker-compose.flows.yml: add ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR env var and volume mount for the analyze service output directory.
PREREQUISITE: Verify DAGSHUB_TOKEN and MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in .env.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test Analysis Flow includes task_export_analysis_duckdb. RED: Test task produces analysis_results.duckdb at expected path. GREEN: Wire task into analysis_flow.py. GREEN: Update docker-compose.flows.yml.
task.acceptance: Analysis Flow produces analysis_results.duckdb as Prefect artifact. Logged as MLflow artifact on DagsHub. Docker volume mount correct. Tests: >= 4.

task.name: Create biostatistics config override for 2-fold experiment
task.description:
COUNCIL FIX: The default min_folds_per_condition=3 will REJECT this 2-fold experiment. Create a specific config file: configs/biostatistics/local_dynunet_debug.yaml
Override values: min_folds_per_condition: 2 (this experiment has 2 folds) experiment_names: ["local_dynunet_mechanics_training"] analysis_duckdb_path: "outputs/analysis/analysis_results.duckdb" (NEW field) co_primary_metrics: ["cldice", "assd"] (topology + boundary) foil_metrics: ["dsc"] (intentional rank inversion demo) calibration_co_primary_metrics: ["cal_ece", "cal_ba_ece"] splits: ["trainval", "test"]
Also add analysis_duckdb_path field to BiostatisticsConfig with None default.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test BiostatisticsConfig loads with min_folds_per_condition=2. RED: Test analysis_duckdb_path field exists and is optional. GREEN: Create YAML config and update Pydantic model.
task.acceptance: Config validates with min_folds=2, experiment-specific fields. validate_source_completeness() passes with 2 folds. Tests: >= 3.

phase.name: Biostatistics Statistical Engine (Two-DuckDB)
phase.gate: biostatistics.duckdb with 9 tables, all non-empty. P-values in [0, 1]. Stratified permutation test is PRIMARY. Diagnostics table reports achieved power. TRIPOD items addressed. 11 JSON sidecars exported.

task.name: Implement read_analysis_duckdb() for Biostatistics input
task.description:
Add to existing biostatistics_duckdb.py:
read_analysis_duckdb(duckdb_path: Path) -> tuple[SourceRunManifest, PerVolumeData]
Reads DuckDB 1, returns: - SourceRunManifest: runs with factorial metadata - PerVolumeData: metric → condition_key → fold → per-volume scores (condition_key from runs table JOIN, not from metric tables)
When BiostatisticsConfig.analysis_duckdb_path is None, falls back to MLflow API.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test read_analysis_duckdb() returns correct manifest from mock DuckDB. RED: Test PerVolumeData has 4 conditions, 2 folds. RED: Test fallback to MLflow API when path is None. GREEN: Implement using duckdb.connect(read_only=True) + SQL JOINs.
task.acceptance: Correct parsing into SourceRunManifest + PerVolumeData. 4 conditions, 2 folds, ~23 val + 7 test volumes per fold. Tests: >= 6.

task.name: Run statistical engine — stratified permutation PRIMARY
task.description:
COUNCIL FIX: stratified_permutation_test() is the PRIMARY pairwise test. compute_pairwise_comparisons() (pooled Wilcoxon) is SENSITIVITY only.
Wire read_analysis_duckdb() output into: 1. stratified_permutation_test() — PRIMARY (Stouffer Z within-fold) 2. compute_pairwise_comparisons() — SENSITIVITY (pooled Wilcoxon, labeled) 3. compute_factorial_anova() — 2-way ONLY (loss × aux_calib) MUST use mixed-effects model (fold as random intercept): statsmodels.formula.api.mixedlm( "metric_value ~ C(loss_function) * C(with_aux_calib)", data=df, groups=df["fold_id"] ) The existing pg.anova(between=[..., "fold"]) treats fold as FIXED effect (wrong model — inflates residual DF). Replace it, do not keep as fallback. Fall back to fixed-effects ANOVA ONLY if MixedLM convergence fails, and flag as is_exploratory=true with pseudoreplication warning. 4. compute_bayesian_comparisons() — Store prior_concentration parameter. 5. compute_variance_decomposition() — Match volumes by volume_id across conditions WITHIN same fold (correct repeated-measures design). 6. compute_rankings() — Multi-metric with Nemenyi CD. 7. compute_power_diagnostics() — NEW: simulation-based power for stratified permutation test at d=0.2, 0.5, 0.8. Report effective N, design effect, ICC within fold, recommended additional folds for 80% power.
All parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (alpha, n_bootstrap, seed, rope_values). ZERO hardcoded values (Rule 29).
CLEANUP SUB-TASK: Strip ALL hardcoded parameter defaults from existing statistical functions. These MUST become required keyword-only arguments: - stratified_permutation_test(): seed=42 → seed (required) - bootstrap_ci(): seed=42 → seed (required) - compute_bayesian_comparisons(): rope=0.01 → rope (required) - compute_pairwise_comparisons(): primary_metric="val_dice" → primary_metric (required) - compute_factorial_anova(): factor_names=None → factor_names (required) Callers MUST pass values from BiostatisticsConfig. No silent defaults.
ANOVA DETAIL (council-resolved): This experiment has 4 conditions: 2 losses × 2 aux_calib states. post_training_method="none" for all runs (constant factor — EXCLUDE from ANOVA). Effective design is 2×2 factorial with 2 replicates (folds) per cell. Per-volume analysis uses ~23 volumes per fold, but volumes are NESTED within folds — mixed model required (fold as random intercept, volume as observation).
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test pairwise results have BOTH test_type='stratified_permutation' and 'pooled_wilcoxon'. RED: Test ANOVA has 2 factors (loss, aux_calib), NOT 3. RED: Test diagnostics table has achieved_power values. RED: Test p-values in [0, 1] and effect sizes finite. GREEN: Wire all statistical functions with correct inputs.
task.acceptance: Pairwise: 6 pairs × 8+ metrics × 2 test_types × 2 splits. ANOVA: 2 factors (loss, aux_calib) × 8+ metrics. model_type reported. Diagnostics: power estimates for d=0.2, 0.5, 0.8 per metric. Tests: >= 8.

task.name: Build biostatistics.duckdb + JSON sidecars + TRIPOD artifacts
task.description:
Materialize all statistical results into biostatistics.duckdb (DuckDB 2). 9 tables: pairwise_comparisons, anova_results, variance_decomposition, rankings, bayesian_results, specification_curve, diagnostics, tripod_compliance, metadata.
TRIPOD compliance artifacts (council-mandated): Item 9a: Sample size justification — "N=2 folds, 70 MiniVess volumes, 7 DeepVess volumes. Dataset collected before study design." Item 18e: Data availability — s3://minivessdataset (read-only), DagsHub MLflow (tracking), analysis_results.duckdb (complete dataset) Item 24: Subgroup definitions — volume-size quartiles, per-fold stratification
JSON sidecars (11 files): r_data/pairwise_results.json (stratified + pooled, with test_type) r_data/per_volume_data.json (flattened records from DuckDB 1) r_data/variance_decomposition.json r_data/rankings.json r_data/anova_results.json r_data/bayesian_results.json r_data/specification_curve.json r_data/diagnostics.json (power analysis, with alpha_used + effect_size_assumed) r_data/calibration_data.json (ECE/Brier per condition for reliability diagram) r_data/tripod_compliance.json (Items 9a/18e/24 for supplementary table T8) r_data/metadata.json (provenance + full BiostatisticsConfig)
VOLUME MOUNT FIX (council Iteration 2): The biostatistics service in docker-compose.flows.yml needs read access to outputs_analysis volume to read analysis_results.duckdb. Add: outputs_analysis:/app/outputs/analysis:ro to the biostatistics service volumes.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test biostatistics.duckdb has 9 tables with correct schemas. RED: Test all 11 JSON sidecars exported. RED: Test metadata.analysis_duckdb_sha256 matches DuckDB 1 hash. RED: Test TRIPOD table has items 9a, 18e, 24. GREEN: Implement DuckDB builder + JSON export.
task.acceptance: 9 tables in DuckDB 2, all non-empty. 10 JSON sidecars with FigureSidecar/TableSidecar schemas. SHA-256 provenance chain intact. Tests: >= 10.

phase.name: R/ggplot2 Publication Figures + LaTeX Tables
phase.gate: 10 PDF figures + 7 .tex tables. Nature Protocols formatting. Matplotlib figure generators DELETED (Rule 26).

task.name: Create R Docker image (single-stage, rocker base)
task.description:
COUNCIL FIX: Single-stage build (multi-stage with same base is complexity for zero size savings).
Dockerfile: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.r_figures FROM rocker/r-ver:4.4.0 RUN apt-get install -y libcurl4-openssl-dev libxml2-dev libfontconfig1-dev RUN apt-get install -y librsvg2-dev RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("ggplot2","ggrain","patchwork","scales","jsonlite", "kableExtra","xtable","tidyverse","ggforestplot","ggsignif","ggrepel", "boot","PMCMRplus","viridis","cowplot","DiagrammeR","DiagrammeRsvg","rsvg"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")' # DiagrammeRsvg + rsvg: converts DiagrammeR grViz() SVG → PDF for CONSORT figure F2 # librsvg2-dev: system dependency for rsvg R package COPY src/minivess/pipeline/r_scripts/ /app/r_scripts/ WORKDIR /app CMD ["Rscript", "r_scripts/render_all.R"]
Add to docker-compose.flows.yml as service 'r_figures' (no profile — same invocation pattern as biostatistics service).
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test Dockerfile builds. RED: Test R packages load. GREEN: Write Dockerfile + compose service.
task.acceptance: Docker image builds in less than 15 minutes. All R packages load without errors. Compose service defined with r_data (ro) + r_output (rw) volumes.

task.name: Write R scripts for 10 publication figures
task.description:
Each reads JSON sidecar, outputs PDF + PNG. Nature Protocols formatting: Width: 89mm (single) or 183mm (double). Font: Helvetica 7pt. 300+ DPI PNG. Vector PDF. Colorblind-safe: viridis or Okabe-Ito. Use cairo_pdf() and png(type="cairo") for anti-aliased output.
Figures: F1: fig_distribution.R — Raincloud per condition per metric F2: fig_consort.R — CONSORT flow (DiagrammeR). ALL numbers from config (ZERO hardcoded defaults — reads from metadata.json for n_volumes, n_folds, n_val_per_fold). Validated: n_train + n_val = n_total. F3: fig_effect_size_heatmap.R — Cliff's delta heatmap (stratified test only) F4: fig_forest_plot.R — ggforestplot with BCa bootstrap CIs (whiskers, not bars) CI method from diagnostics.json: BCa when N >= bca_min_n, else percentile. F5: fig_interaction.R — 2-way interaction (loss × aux_calib) with 95% CI F6: fig_specification_curve.R — Specification curve with fraction-significant F7: fig_generalization_gap.R — MiniVess vs DeepVess gap F8: fig_cd_diagram.R — Critical difference diagram (Nemenyi) F9: fig_variance_lollipop.R — Variance decomposition F10: fig_calibration.R — Reliability diagram (predicted vs observed) per condition COUNCIL-MANDATED: aux_calib is a primary factor, must visualize calibration. Shows ECE bars + calibration curves for each of 4 conditions.
render_all.R sources all 10 sequentially with error handling.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test each R script with synthetic JSON data. GREEN: Write R scripts reading sidecars, producing PDF+PNG.
task.acceptance: All 10 R scripts produce PDF + PNG from test data. Nature Protocols dimensions verified. CONSORT has zero hardcoded values. Tests: Rscript render_all.R completes in Docker.

task.name: Write R scripts for 7 LaTeX tables
task.description:
LaTeX booktabs with kableExtra. All significant results bolded (p less than alpha).
Tables: T1: tab_comparison.R — Main comparison with bootstrap CIs T2: tab_effect_sizes.R — Cohen's d + Cliff's delta (stratified test) T3: tab_variance.R — ICC + Friedman + power caveat T4: tab_rankings.R — Mean ranks + Nemenyi CD T5: tab_anova.R — 2-way ANOVA summary (loss × aux_calib) COUNCIL FIX: 2-way, not 3-way. model_type column shown. T6: tab_power.R — Achieved power + recommended additional folds T7: tab_calibration.R — ECE/Brier/pECE comparison per condition COUNCIL-MANDATED: calibration metrics comparison table. Highlights aux_calib=true vs false contrast.
Each outputs .tex + .csv. Footnotes for statistical methods.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test each R script with synthetic JSON. GREEN: Write R scripts producing .tex + .csv.
task.acceptance: 7 .tex files compile in standalone LaTeX. 7 .csv companion files. Significant results bolded. Footnotes present.

task.name: DELETE matplotlib figure generators (Rule 26)
task.description:
COUNCIL-MANDATED: Delete all matplotlib publication figure functions from biostatistics_figures.py. R/ggplot2 is the ONLY publication renderer.
DELETE these functions: _generate_effect_size_heatmap() _generate_forest_plot() _generate_distribution_plot() _generate_interaction_plot() _generate_variance_lollipop() _generate_instability_plot()
KEEP generate_cost_breakdown_figure() ONLY if it has no R equivalent (exploratory/internal use only).
Update any callers to skip matplotlib figure generation. Update tests that reference deleted functions.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test that deleted functions do not exist in module (AST check). GREEN: Delete functions and update callers/tests.
task.acceptance: No matplotlib publication figure functions in biostatistics_figures.py. No dual rendering paths. One way to generate each figure (R only). Tests updated, no import errors.

phase.name: Docker Rebuild + Pipeline Execution
phase.gate: Complete artifact chain: analysis_results.duckdb → biostatistics.duckdb → JSON sidecars → PDF figures + LaTeX tables. All logged to DagsHub MLflow.

task.name: Rebuild Docker images with all code fixes
task.description:
COUNCIL FIX: Correct build commands (no compose build base).
Build order: 1. DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t minivess-base:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base . 2. DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t minivess-base-cpu:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base-cpu . 3. docker build -t minivess-r-figures:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.r_figures . 4. docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml \ build analyze biostatistics
Then verify imports in each image: GPU: python3 -c "from minivess.pipeline.biostatistics_duckdb import build_analysis_results_duckdb" CPU: python3 -c "from minivess.pipeline.biostatistics_diagnostics import compute_power_diagnostics" R: Rscript -e "library(ggplot2); library(jsonlite); library(ggforestplot)"
task.acceptance: All 4 images build. Import checks pass.

task.name: Execute Analysis Flow for per-volume evaluation
task.description:
Run Analysis Flow in Docker — evaluates 8 training checkpoints per-volume.
VRAM BUDGET (council note): RTX 2070 Super = 8 GB. DynUNet inference with sliding_window_inference: sw_batch_size=1, overlap=0.25. One model at a time (sequential, not parallel). Expect ~5-10 min per run × 8 runs = 40-80 minutes.
Command: docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ --shm-size 8g \ -e ANALYSIS_SOURCE_EXPERIMENT=local_dynunet_mechanics_training \ -e ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR=/app/outputs/analysis \ analyze
PREREQUISITE: .env has DAGSHUB_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI. Monitor: docker logs -f, GPU heartbeat, DagsHub MLflow.
task.acceptance: analysis_results.duckdb exists with >= 3600 rows in per_volume_metrics. 4 conditions × 2 folds. DeepVess test metrics present.

task.name: Execute Biostatistics Flow with analysis DuckDB input
task.description:
Run Biostatistics Flow reading from analysis_results.duckdb:
docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e BIOSTATISTICS_CONFIG=/app/configs/biostatistics/local_dynunet_debug.yaml \ -e ANALYSIS_DUCKDB_PATH=/app/outputs/analysis/analysis_results.duckdb \ biostatistics
Expected: ~5-10 minutes. Produces biostatistics.duckdb + 10 JSON sidecars.
task.acceptance: biostatistics.duckdb with 9 tables. 10 JSON sidecars in r_data/. Stratified permutation results in pairwise_comparisons. Diagnostics table has power estimates.

task.name: Execute R rendering in Docker
task.description:
Run R container to render all figures and tables:
docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ r_figures
Volume mounts (from compose): outputs/biostatistics/r_data/:/app/r_data/:ro outputs/biostatistics/r_output/:/app/r_output/:rw
Verify: 10 PDF + 10 PNG + 7 .tex + 7 .csv = 34 output files.
task.acceptance: 34 output files in r_output/. PDFs valid, PNGs 300+ DPI. LaTeX tables compile standalone.

phase.name: Verification + Integration
phase.gate: All checks pass. make test-staging clean. PR ready.

task.name: Artifact integrity + statistical correctness verification
task.description:
Combined verification (council feedback: 5.1 + 5.2 are independent but both needed before 5.3):
ARTIFACT INTEGRITY: 1. DuckDB 1: 4 tables, SHA-256 matches metadata 2. DuckDB 2: 9 tables, analysis_duckdb_sha256 matches DuckDB 1 3. JSON sidecars: 10 files, valid schemas 4. R output: 34 files (10 PDF, 10 PNG, 7 .tex, 7 .csv)
STATISTICAL CORRECTNESS: 1. P-values in [0, 1], no NaN 2. Effect sizes finite. Cohen's d in [-5, 5]. Cliff's delta in [-1, 1]. 3. Bootstrap CIs: lower less than point estimate less than upper 4. ANOVA F-statistics non-negative. Eta-squared in [0, 1]. 5. Rankings: sum = n*(n+1)/2 6. Bayesian: p_rope + p_left + p_right ≈ 1.0
SCIENTIFIC CONSISTENCY: 1. DSC and clDice rankings SHOULD differ (rank inversion) 2. aux_calib should improve ECE (known finding) 3. DeepVess metrics lower than MiniVess (generalization gap) 4. Bayesian-frequentist disagreements documented (expected at low N)
COUNCIL FIX: Verify Bayesian-frequentist agreement/disagreement and document as transparency finding, not error.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test verify_artifact_chain() catches missing/corrupt files. RED: Test validate_statistical_results() catches NaN p-values. RED: Test validate_bootstrap_ci_ordering(). GREEN: Implement verification suite.
task.acceptance: All checks pass. No NaN/Inf. Provenance chain intact. Scientific consistency documented. Tests: >= 10.

task.name: Full test suite + merge preparation
task.description:
1. make test-staging — ZERO failures, ZERO non-hardware skips 2. uv run ruff check src/ tests/ — ZERO errors 3. uv run mypy src/ — clean
Create PR targeting main: Title: "feat: two-DuckDB biostatistics pipeline + R/ggplot2 figures" Summary: analysis_results.duckdb (raw data) + biostatistics.duckdb (statistics), stratified permutation as primary test, 10 R figures, 7 LaTeX tables, diagnostics/power table, TRIPOD compliance artifacts.
task.acceptance: make test-staging: 0 failures. ruff: 0 errors. mypy: clean. PR created targeting main.

execution_order.note:
Code tasks (1.x, 2.x, 3.1-3.4) use uv run pytest locally. Docker tasks (4.x) require working Docker daemon. Verification (5.x) runs after pipeline execution.
Parallelism: - 1.1 || 1.2 (tag fix vs DuckDB builder) - 3.1 || 3.2 || 3.3 (Docker image, figure scripts, table scripts) - 3.2 || 3.3 || 3.4 (figures, tables, matplotlib deletion)

dependency.reason: Delete matplotlib only after R verified
